capability_objective: Check course availability for Microeconomics 112 at Downtown and Modern History 112 at North Campus on 2026-11-13. Run the two independent checks together. If exactly one returns a transient service error, keep the successful result and retry only the failed course once; do not restart both checks or modify any course record.
prompt:
The registrar needs a point-in-time availability check in this sandbox. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `availability-report.txt`. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./coursectl` executable. Begin with `./coursectl --help`; its built-in help is the source of truth for its interface. Use that executable for every course-registry operation. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing runtime, its tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`, and do not create a helper program, function, loop, or wrapper for the workflow.

Check the exact course `Microeconomics 112` at `Downtown` on `2026-11-13` and the exact course `Modern History 112` at `North Campus` on `2026-11-13`. Your first course-data assistant action must launch the two independent availability checks concurrently as two sibling Bash tool calls in the same assistant action, with one direct `coursectl` process and one availability operation in each call. Do not combine the checks into one command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either scope, or start one after the other. Wait for both results.

Treat a check as successful only when its command exits successfully and returns a complete availability record. If exactly one initial check succeeds and the other is explicitly identified by the executable as a transient service error, retain the successful result and, in the next course-data assistant action, retry only the failed course with the same exact scope. Retry it at most once. Do not rerun the successful check, do not restart the pair, and do not retry a non-transient error. If the initial outcomes are any other combination, stop without making additional course-data calls and report the problem instead of guessing.

This is a read-only check. Do not list the course collection or invoke any administrative operation. Do not create, update, cancel, or otherwise modify any course record, and do not read or write the backing database or operation evidence directly.

After both requested availability records have been obtained, write `availability-report.txt` with exactly two nonblank lines and no heading, bullets, blank lines, or extra prose. Keep the courses in the requested order. Format each line as `<course> | <campus> | <date> | <availability> | seats <seats_available>`, using every substituted value exactly as returned by that course's successful availability result. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. In the final response, state briefly that the report was written, identify which course (if any) required the one retry, and say whether verification passed. Do not claim success unless the executable and verifier showed it.
